title: Non-Amazon US Scope and Catalog Guardrails - 2026-05-04

messages.text: Asked Codex what scrapes were in the pipeline, then instructed Codex to replace bfreeaustralia.com with https://www.bfreeusa.com/ so non-Amazon scraping focuses on products that ship to US customers.

Updated local lead/inventory CSVs to replace the B Free Australia storefront with the B Free US storefront. Added scrape_bfreeusa_reviews.py using Shopify products.json plus the public Stamped reviews endpoint, scoped out baby and non-clothing products, and ran the scrape.

BFree US final metrics: 1193 products discovered/scanned, 426 rows written, 342 distinct reviews, 426 distinct images, 90 distinct product URLs, 88 rows with size, 9 rows with any measurement, 6 image+product+size+measurement qualified rows, and 0 baby rows.
Asked for the next scrape. Codex identified Margovil as an adapter-build candidate because the generic scraper had previously attached aggregate Judge.me review media to unrelated products.

Added scrape_margovil_reviews.py. The scraper discovers the Shopify catalog through products.json and calls only the public product-level Judge.me reviews_for_widget endpoint. It explicitly avoids the all_reviews aggregate fallback and only accepts review blocks matching the current product URL.

Margovil final metrics: 1765 products discovered/scanned, 126 rows written, 51 distinct reviews, 126 distinct images, 38 distinct products, 6 rows with size, 18 rows with any measurement, 2 image+product+size+measurement qualified rows, aggregate_review_feed_used false, and 18 product-widget checks hit HTTP 429.
Asked for the next scrape. Codex ran SwimOutlet through the generic scraper using --only-seed-products as a smoke test.

SwimOutlet seed scrape used the Okendo adapter and scanned only two seed URLs from triage. The homepage seed was skipped out of scope; the product seed produced 12 rows from one review with 12 images. Inventory was initially marked scraped, then corrected after the user asked why the row count was so low.
Asked what needed to change so all future scrapes scan every product page, not just the given lead URL or a summary.
Explained the new operating rule: lead URLs are seeds, not scope. A scrape is complete only when it discovers and scans the full product catalog or documents why discovery is unavailable.

Updated scrape_p0_lead_reviews.py to stamp every run with seed_url_count, seed_urls, discovery_method, catalog_discovery_attempted, products_discovered, product_pages_scanned, scrape_scope_status, full_catalog_scrape_complete, seed_scrape_only, and aggregate_feed_used. Seed-only mode now requires --allow-incomplete-seed-scrape and writes a warning that the retailer should not be marked fully scraped.

Updated summarize_non_amazon_scrapes.py so report CSV and Markdown include product discovery, product pages scanned, seed count, discovery method, scrape scope status, full-catalog completion, and aggregate-feed usage. Corrected SwimOutlet inventory to scrape_status seed scrape only with notes that it needs a full product discovery adapter.
Asked Codex to update git, S3, and the transcripts table.

metadata.scope: Non-Amazon US storefront replacement, product-level scraper additions, and future full-catalog scrape guardrails
notes: Do not mark seed-only outputs as fully scraped.
Production non-Amazon scrapes should discover and scan the full product catalog.
Aggregate review feeds are only acceptable if product URLs are reliably preserved and not assigned to unrelated products.
